content:
NSERC Funding Programs

{/* Idea to Innovation (I2I) Grants */}
Idea to Innovation (I2I) Grants

Phase I: Reduction-to-Practice

Funding Amount: Up to $125K

• Duration: Up to 12 months

• Coverage: 100% of direct research costs

• Focus: Proof-of-concept validation

• Renewable: Non-renewable

Phase IIa: Early-Stage Investment

Funding Amount: Up to $125K/year

• Duration: 6-18 months

• NSERC Coverage: 67% of costs

• Partner Contribution: 33% (cash)

• Focus: Technology enhancement

Phase IIb: Company Partnership

Funding Amount: Up to $350K

• Duration: Up to 24 months

• NSERC Coverage: 50% of costs

• Partner Contribution: 50% (40% cash min)

• Focus: Commercialization readiness

⚠️ Current Status (2026):

Active Programs: Phase I, IIa, and IIb applications accepted

Application Deadlines: Quarterly - Jan 8, Mar 31, Jun 20, Sep 22

Paused Programs: Market Assessment and Phase Ib (until further notice)

Reason: Program review and process optimization

{/* Collaborative Research and Development */}
Collaborative Research and Development (CRD)

CRD Grants

Funding Range: $20K - $500K/year

• University-industry research partnerships

• Matching funding required from partners

• Duration: Up to 5 years

• Focus on applied research challenges

Industrial Research Chairs

Funding: Up to $500K/year for 5 years

Purpose: Long-term industry-university research partnerships

Requirements: Minimum 1:1 industry matching

Benefit: Dedicated research program leadership

{/* Strategic Partnership Grants */}
Strategic Partnership Grants
Networks

• Large-scale research networks

• Multi-institutional collaboration

• Up to $2.5M/year

• 5-7 year terms

Centres

• Research excellence centres

• Strategic priority areas

• Significant infrastructure

• Long-term commitments

Initiatives

• Targeted research themes

• National priorities

• Cross-sectoral partnerships

• Innovation ecosystems
shortAnswer: To apply for NSERC Research Grants Canada 2026, start by reviewing the eligibility criteria and preparing a project proposal. Complete guide to NSERC research grants including Idea to Innovation (I2I) grants up to $350K. University-industry partnerships and technology transfer funding. Funding available: up to $350K.
shortAnswerQuestion: How can my business apply for NSERC Research Grants Canada 2026 in 2026?

faq.question: Is NSERC only for university professors?
faq.answer: While NSERC Discovery Grants target academics, programs like Alliance Grants are designed specifically to fund R&D partnerships between Canadian businesses and academic researchers.

faq.question: How much does a business have to contribute to an NSERC Alliance Grant?
faq.answer: It depends on the company size. Small-to-medium enterprises (SMEs) typically contribute between 10% and 33% of the project cost in cash, while NSERC covers the remaining 67% to 90%.

faq.question: Does NSERC fund software development?
faq.answer: Only if the software involves fundamental research or addressing significant technical uncertainty. NSERC does not fund routine software engineering, UI design, or simple app development.

expertTip.title: University Affiliation Required
expertTip.content: NSERC grants require a principal investigator at a Canadian university. If you're a startup, partner with a professor who can be your PI.
